response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist web and full-stack engineer, but the match to this role is poor. The position requires native-level Japanese, deep C/UNIX development, Linux kernel source-code analysis and modification, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking). The resume shows none of these: language skills are English, Romanian, and basic German; C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief 7-month stint over a decade ago; and career focus has been web platforms, TypeScript/Node.js, and D-language services rather than embedded firmware or network security.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with strong testing culture (TDD, test tooling authorship)
Some systems-level exposure: DLang low-latency services, C/C++ at National Instruments, Linux proficiency
Demonstrated ownership and open-source leadership (GISCollective, test frameworks)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — an explicit hard requirement
No embedded networking, switch, or security-technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
No Linux kernel investigation/modification or C-for-UNIX depth in recent 10+ years of work

key_factors.factor: General seniority and systems programming background
